subject: Physics
title: The First Black Hole Photo
description: A micro-lesson that makes The First Black Hole Photo usable.

hook.text: In April 2019, humanity saw a black hole for the first time—a glowing ring around a dark shadow.

buildup.text: The Event Horizon Telescope linked eight radio dishes worldwide to form one Earth-sized telescope.

discovery.text: They imaged M87's supermassive black hole, 55 million light-years away and 6.5 billion solar masses.

twist.text: The image matched general relativity's predictions perfectly. Einstein would have smiled.

climax.text: In 2022, they captured Sagittarius A*—the black hole at the center of our own Milky Way.

punchline.text: Eight telescopes, one planet, one photo. We saw the invisible.

quiz.question: How was the first image of a black hole captured?
options: By linking eight telescopes worldwide into one Earth-sized dish
By sending a probe near the black hole
By using the Hubble Space Telescope
